commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: dashboard filter pills CSS class mismatch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

core: wire TransferJournal into download/upload for automatic resume (#519)

Integrate TransferJournal into DeviceActor transfer paths with four
key fixes for resume reliability:

- Preserve temp files on read failure when journal is active, enabling
  resume from the last flushed offset instead of starting over
- Add ETag validation (file-size check) before resuming, detecting
  server-side changes that would corrupt a resumed transfer
- Use actual file size on disk as resume offset instead of journal's
  committedBytes, which may be stale if interrupted mid-flush
- Check device operationsSupported for GetPartialObject (0x95C1) in
  addition to quirks policy flags for partial resume capability

Add MTPError.etagMismatch with full error messaging chain
(diagnostic, actionable, CLI). Add 10 tests covering ETag validation,
temp file preservation, progress tracking, and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add motor runaway and power-loss FMEA tests (#103)

Add 40 comprehensive FMEA tests covering critical safety gaps:

- Motor runaway detection and torque clamping (3 tests)
- Runaway mitigation: force ramp to zero, emergency stop (3 tests)
- Current/torque limiting at configured max levels (5 tests)
- Stall detection: overcurrent on zero-speed motor (3 tests)
- Power loss simulation and graceful degradation (3 tests)
- Brownout recovery and safe re-initialization (3 tests)
- Watchdog timeout enforcement and safe state (5 tests)
- Concurrent safety events handling (4 tests)
- Recovery after fault with manual reset (5 tests)
- Torque direction validation and mismatch detection (6 tests)

All tests follow project conventions:
- No unwrap()/expect() — Result-returning tests
- Uses existing SafetyService, SafetyInterlockSystem, TorqueLimit,
  WatchdogTimeoutHandler, and VirtualDevice test infrastructure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
quirks: research and implement additional libmtp compat flags (#518)

Add 6 new QuirkFlags from libmtp device-flags.h analysis:
- only7BitFilenames (DEVICE_FLAG_ONLY_7BIT_FILENAMES)
- requireUniqueFilenames (DEVICE_FLAG_UNIQUE_FILENAMES)
- cannotHandleDateModified (DEVICE_FLAG_CANNOT_HANDLE_DATEMODIFIED)
- brokenBatteryLevel (DEVICE_FLAG_BROKEN_BATTERY_LEVEL)
- alwaysProbeDescriptor (DEVICE_FLAG_ALWAYS_PROBE_DESCRIPTOR)
- deleteSendsEvent (DEVICE_FLAG_DELETE_SENDS_EVENT)

Wire only7BitFilenames into PathSanitizer.sanitizeForMTP() and
DeviceActor write path. Full Codable support with backward-compat
defaults. Update MTPCompatibilityResearch.md with expanded coverage
analysis (22 of 32 libmtp flags now covered).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SwiftMTP-dev
repository.description: Swift-native MacOS and iOS MTP library

commit.message:
docs: Pixel 7 bulk timeout deep analysis + quirks update (#517)

Add comprehensive root cause analysis for the Pixel 7 (18d1:4ee1) bulk
transfer timeout issue. Key findings:

- libmtp uses double libusb_reset_device() (FORCE_RESET_ON_CLOSE) while
  SwiftMTP does a single reset — the second reset may be required for
  macOS/Darwin USBDeviceReEnumerate to fully flush IOKit state
- Android FunctionFS-based MTP server needs 70-250ms to reinitialize
  endpoints after USB bus reset
- libmtp uses 60,000ms operational timeout (LONG_TIMEOUT flag) vs
  SwiftMTP's 30,000ms
- libmtp skips set_interface_alt_setting on macOS (#ifndef __APPLE__)

Quirks changes (google-pixel-7-4ee1):
- ioTimeoutMs: 30000 → 60000 (match libmtp LONG_TIMEOUT)
- Add forceDoubleReset flag (match libmtp FORCE_RESET_ON_CLOSE)
- Add longTimeout flag

New file: Docs/pixel7-bulk-timeout-analysis.md
Updated: Specs/quirks.json, SwiftMTPQuirks/Resources/quirks.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SwiftMTP-dev

commit.message:
fix(coc): prevent hover loop on mobile for tool call BottomSheet

On touch devices, synthetic mouse events from touch interactions caused
a hover loop: touchstart triggered mouseenter, which started the 300ms
timer to show the BottomSheet, then touchend fired mouseleave which
dismissed it — repeatedly cycling.

Fix:
- Import useBreakpoint in ToolCallView and call it inside the component
- Only attach onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave to the header when !isMobile
- Add a mobile-only 👁 preview button that immediately sets hoverVisible=true
  on tap, avoiding any interaction with the hover timer machinery
- The BottomSheet close path (onMouseLeave → setHoverVisible(false)) is
  unchanged, so dismissal still works correctly

Tests: 7 new tests in ToolCallView-hover.test.tsx covering
- mobile preview button render/hide
- immediate BottomSheet on tap (no delay)
- single render (no flicker)
- hover events suppressed on mobile
- expand/collapse still works
- tap preview does not trigger expand

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use full sequence for n_codons=1 to capture INSERTION_POS=5266

For n_codons=1, max_nodes=64 → use_len=192 nt — far short of
INSERTION_POS=5266. The carrier's mutant codon (at ~nt 5265) was
never loaded into the node table, so target_found=False and the
6-qubit carrier search always failed.

For n_codons=1 there are at most 4^3=64 unique 3-nt windows, which
is exactly the full 2^6=64 statevector space. Using the full
sequence is therefore safe (unique nodes stay ≤64) and necessary.

Fix applied to all three use-sites:
  - bio_grover_local (carrier/healthy search)
  - bio_grover_ibm_submit (QPU path)
  - bio_circuit_info (history step-circuit rebuild)

n_codons=2 and n_codons=3 are unaffected (use_len already covers 5266).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Apply suggestion from @Copilot

commit.message:
index: add FTS5 full-text search for device file index (#514)

Add FTS5 virtual table for fast filename and path search across
device content. Include trigger-based sync and safe query escaping.

- FTS5 virtual table (objects_fts) synced via INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE triggers
- searchByFilename() and searchByPath() methods with FTS5 MATCH
- escapeFTS5Query() for safe user input handling
- rebuildFTSIndex() for post-migration rebuilds
- 16 new tests covering search, escaping, limits, stale exclusion
- Adjusted VACUUM test threshold for FTS5 shadow table overhead

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: nix print-dev-env キャッシュで nix develop を高速化

nix-direnv と同じアプローチで、nix develop の代わり nix print-dev-env を利用する。
2回目以降は source するだけで起動する。

- entrypoint.sh: flake.nix+flake.lock のハッシュをキーにキャッシュ
  - 初回: nix print-dev-env --profile でGCルート付きキャッシュ生成
  - 2回目以降: cached env を source して即座にエージェント起動
  - print-dev-env 失敗時は従来の nix develop にフォールバック
- mount.ts: XDG_CACHE_HOME/nas をコンテナにマウントしてキャッシュを永続化

nix コマンド実行時の権限問題も修正:
- Git safe.directory にワークスペースパスを追加
- ホストの XDG_CACHE_HOME/nix もマウントして Permission denied を回避
- chown でコンテナ内の $HOME ディレクトリをコンテナユーザーが所有するよう設定

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test: harden service integration tests (#100)

Add 44 new integration tests in service_hardening_tests.rs covering:

- Device service: multi-device enumeration, state queries, re-enumeration,
  empty port, health checks, device status, list_devices alias
- Game service: active game lifecycle, support lookup, telemetry mapping,
  stable/experimental partition, config writing, unsupported game errors,
  game ID alias normalization, writer BDD metrics, concurrent queries
- Profile service: signed profiles, load-by-string, update nonexistent,
  clear active profile, list all, hierarchy fallback to global, session
  override priority, statistics with signed profiles
- Diagnostic service: all registered tests runnable, full suite count,
  system requirements metadata, timing test
- Safety service: wrong fault type clear, non-faulted clear, respond
  without challenge, unregistered device errors, sequential fault count,
  critical fault torque halving, concurrent fault reporting, hands-on toggle
- Anti-cheat: report generation, markdown output, JSON serialization
- Cross-service: concurrent profile/device/safety operations

All tests use Result-returning pattern (no unwrap/expect), are independent,
and use virtual devices and temp directories.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: expand telemetry pipeline test coverage (#99)

Add 104 new tests across four telemetry crates covering previously untested
pipeline paths:

- telemetry-core (39 tests): timestamp monotonicity, serde roundtrips for
  all domain types, ConnectionState transitions, DisconnectionTracker
  behavior, TelemetryError variants, and property-based GameTelemetry tests.

- telemetry-adapters (24 tests): adapter factory registry validation,
  Forza adapter payload edge cases (empty/minimal/maximal/malformed),
  gear encoding, G-force parsing, extended fields, serialization roundtrips,
  and property-based fuzz testing of normalize() with random payloads.

- telemetry-recorder (17 tests): save/load data integrity roundtrips,
  flag preservation, error paths, empty recording handling, timestamp
  monotonicity in fixtures, all TestScenario variants, TelemetryPlayer
  edge cases, and large recording save/load.

- telemetry-config (24 tests): normalize_game_id edge cases, matrix
  loading validation, field mapping completeness, GameSupportMatrix
  methods, JSON roundtrips, config writer factory validation, and
  property-based game ID normalization tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

   package.json from main (before merge) instead of preview

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7482)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Remove passthrough ast-contract.ts wrapper; call Core.isFunctionLikeNode directly

The `ast-contract.ts` file in `src/lint/src/doc-comment/` was a pure
re-export wrapper: it imported `isFunctionLikeNode` from `@gml-modules/core`
and re-exposed it under the alias `isFunctionLikeDocCommentNode` with no
additional logic.  This violated two explicit architectural contracts:

1. AGENTS.md: "Do NOT create re-export wrappers. Files should always import
   the specific functions or values they need directly at the point of use."
2. The file's own TODO comment: "Remove this passthrough function and file
   and just call Core.isFunctionLikeNode directly."

Fix: remove `ast-contract.ts` and update the single consumer
(`synthetic-helpers.ts`) to call `Core.isFunctionLikeNode(node)` directly,
using the `Core` namespace import that was already present in the file.

Boundary enforced: lint/doc-comment internal modules must not create
indirection layers around core utilities.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent: Target State Alignment (#7499)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce formatter/parser boundary: remove dormant legacy @function source-text fallback

The formatter's printNodeDocComments previously contained a dead fallback
(lines 283-295 of print.ts) that read raw source text and converted legacy
// @function doc comment formats to /// @function, then replaced the AST's
docComments with these converted lines whenever the AST lacked an @function
tag. This was:

  - A content rewrite (format conversion) that belongs in @gml-modules/lint
    (gml/normalize-doc-comments rule), per target-state.md §2.2 and §3.2.
  - Dead code — no test fixtures exercise this path (no // @function legacy
    format fixtures exist in the test suite).
  - Superseded by the parser's normalizeFunctionDocCommentAttachments pass
    (src/parser/src/ast/normalize-function-doc-comment-attachments.ts), which
    pre-attaches recognised @function-tag comments to the correct function node
    before the formatter ever runs.

Changes:
1. print.ts: Remove the legacy @function source-text fallback block from
   printNodeDocComments. The formatter now trusts node.docComments exclusively
   (target-state.md §2.2, §3.2, §3.5).

2. function-parameter-naming-boundary.test.ts: Fix stale expected exports list
   that still included filterMisattachedFunctionDocComments (another dormant
   parser-workaround that was previously migrated out of the format workspace).
   Updated to expect only ["findEnclosingFunctionDeclaration",
   "joinDeclaratorPartsWithCommas"]. Added an explicit guard test with a comment
   documenting the migration history, so the function cannot be silently
   re-introduced (target-state.md §3.5).

3. formatter-boundaries-ownership.test.ts: Add regression-guard test
   "does not upgrade legacy double-slash @function to triple-slash" to confirm
   the formatter no longer converts // @function to /// @function. That
   conversion belongs exclusively to the lint workspace.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: robpitcher <rob@youforgeit.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract audio streaming demo into separate functions

- Split main() into focused helper functions for clarity
- createUDPTransport(): Initialize UDP transport
- createRTPIntegration(): Setup RTP integration layer
- createToxAVManager(): Create ToxAV manager with adapters
- startCall(): Initiate audio call with friend
- setupMediaPipeline(): Configure media streaming
- sendAudioFrames(): Handle audio frame transmission
- displayCallStatistics(): Show call metrics
- endCall(): Terminate active call
- displaySummary(): Output session summary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix crash: configure Firebase via AppDelegate before StateObject init

FirebaseApp.configure() must run before Auth.auth() is accessed.
Using UIApplicationDelegateAdaptor ensures correct initialization order.

commit.message:
docs: update roadmap — move browser recording to upcoming, fix badge statuses

Browser recording was incorrectly listed as shipped (✅). Moved it to
an Upcoming section with 'In Development' badge. Downgraded AI Refinement
and Script Editor from 'In Progress' to 'Planned' to reflect actual status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: migrate provisioning from bash to Ansible

- Rewrite install.sh as a bootstrap shim (Xcode CLT → Homebrew →
  Ansible → ansible-playbook). Usage: ./install.sh [work|personal]

- Add ansible/ playbook with 5 roles:
  - bootstrap: Xcode CLT, Rosetta 2, Homebrew, Oh My Zsh, SSH key gen,
    git global config, chsh to zsh
  - homebrew: runs brew bundle against existing Brewfile
  - dotfiles: deploys .zshrc from Jinja2 template (work/personal split),
    symlinks .vimrc/.p10k.zsh/.editorconfig/.eslintrc.json, copies
    iTerm2 DynamicProfile
  - macos: idempotent defaults write for trackpad, keyboard, mouse,
    Dock, Finder, screenshots — values captured from current machine
  - lazyvim: clones LazyVim/starter to ~/.config/nvim, headless plugin
    sync; deps (ripgrep, fd, lazygit, rust, lua-language-server) added
    to Brewfile

- Add Ansible Vault: work secrets (NPM_TOKEN, GRADLEW creds, all
  THD_ID_OIDC_* vars) encrypted in group_vars/work/vault.yml.
  .vault_pass is gitignored.

- Separate inventory/work and inventory/personal with per-group vars
  driving machine_type in the .zshrc Jinja2 template

- Brewfile: add ableton-live-suite, ripgrep, fd, lazygit, rust,
  lua-language-server; remove duplicate cask entries (visual-studio-code,
  macvim)

- Update README with new provisioning workflow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent duplicate activities (same proyecto, tipo, fecha, hora)

Check for existing activity with matching programa_id, tipo_id, fecha,
and hora_inicio before insert/update. Shows inline error message on
the fecha field if a duplicate is found.
